question.text: At any point in time, how many monthly equity derivative contracts are available for trading?

answer.text: Three — near month, next month, and far month
One — only the current month's contract
Six — contracts for the next six months
Twelve — contracts for the next twelve months

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** At any time, three monthly contracts are available: (a) NEAR month — expiring on the last Tuesday or Thursday of the current month, (b) NEXT month — expiring the following month, and (c) FAR month — expiring in the third month. Additionally, quarterly and semi-annual contracts are also available for longer maturities.
**The Trap:** It is not just one month or six months — exactly three monthly contracts are always available. When the near month contract expires, a new far month contract is introduced. The exchange also specifies quarterly and semi-annual contracts separately.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Derivatives basics overlap with Series VIII, XIII. Understanding the contract cycle is essential for all derivatives exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Near month contracts are the most actively traded and liquid. As a result, they have the tightest spreads and lowest impact costs. Most retail traders should stick to near month contracts for better liquidity.
